chief_complaint: A 21-year-old female presented with a febrile illness followed by refractory status epilepticus.
history: The patient had a febrile illness 5 days prior to admission, which was initially treated with antipyretics. She then developed refractory status epilepticus, requiring multiple antiepileptic medications and intubation. The patient had no significant past medical history, was not taking any medications, and had no known allergies.
exam: Vital signs: temperature 38.5°C, blood pressure 120/80 mmHg, heart rate 120 bpm, respiratory rate 24 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed a lethargic patient with no focal neurological deficits.
labs: MRI brain scan: abnormal findings in the temporal lobe. ECG: normal. Bloods: normal. Other tests: none.
differentials: Refractory status epilepticus due to unknown cause
correct_ranking: Refractory status epilepticus due to unknown cause
